The finding is about ensuring PAM enforces password requirements, specifically the retry parameter in /etc/security/pwquality.conf. The rule is accounts_password_pam_pwquality_retry (Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_accounts_password_pam_pwquality_retry).

The description says: "To configure the number of retry prompts that are permitted per-session: Edit the /ec/security/pwquality.conf to include retry= , or a lower value if site policy is more restrictive. The profile requirement is a maximum of retry= prompts per session."

It seems like we need to set the retry value in /etc/security/pwquality.conf. The remediation script snippet shows that they want to set var_password_pam_retry to some value (likely 3 or something). But we need to check what the actual requirement is. Typically, the STIG or security baseline might require a specific value. ```
# Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms
if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then
    var_password_pam_retry="3"
    if grep -sq retry /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then
        sed -i "/retry/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf

    stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^retry")

        sed -i "s/^$stripped_key.*/$formatted_output/" /etc/security/pwquality.conf
    else
        printf "\n%s\n" "$formatted_output" >> /etc/security/pwquality.conf
    fi
